role_title: Operations Program Manager - Robotics Data Acquisition

level_signals: 3-5 years of hands-on experience in manufacturing, industrial / process engineering, operations, robotics, or production environments
Coordinate across floor operators and technicians with limited supervision
Hands-on floor presence role, not a people manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Coordinate daily operations readiness across workstations, operators, materials.
Track core operating metrics including utilization, cycle time, throughput, downtime, operator productivity, and data quality.
Identify bottlenecks through workflow analysis, time studies, and capacity modeling, then drive practical fixes.
Execute the rollout of new hardware, sensors, tools, and process changes with Engineering, Operations, Facilities, Supply Chain, and Safety.
Identify equipment readiness issues and coordinate with technical support to keep workstations, and test equipment calibrated, configured, maintained, and ready for rollouts and evaluations.
Lead root cause analysis for recurring operational issues and follow through on corrective actions.
Provide operation input to create and maintain SOPs, work instructions, training materials, and process controls.
Identify and flag resource constraints and manage issue escalation and resolution.
Bring 3-5 years of hands-on experience in manufacturing, industrial / process engineering, operations, robotics, or production environments.
Have experience using Lean, Six Sigma, time studies, or similar methods to improve processes.
Understand operating KPIs such as cycle time, takt time, throughput, utilization, yield, OEE, and uptime.
Can manage multiple work-streams, coordinate across floor operators and technicians to drive execution with limited supervision.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have a bachelor's degree in Industrial, Mechanical, Manufacturing, or a related technical field.
Have exposure to robotics data acquisition, autonomous vehicle systems, camera / audio systems, automation, or robotics systems.

scope.surface: Day-to-day operations of robotics data acquisition / data collection facilities
scope.business_model: internal AI research operations (robotics data collection infrastructure)
core_responsibilities: Coordinate daily operations readiness across workstations, operators, materials
Track core operating metrics including utilization, cycle time, throughput, downtime, operator productivity, and data quality
Identify bottlenecks through workflow analysis, time studies, and capacity modeling, then drive practical fixes
Execute the rollout of new hardware, sensors, tools, and process changes
Identify equipment readiness issues and coordinate with technical support
Lead root cause analysis for recurring operational issues and follow through on corrective actions
Provide operation input to create and maintain SOPs, work instructions, training materials, and process controls
Identify and flag resource constraints and manage issue escalation and resolution

green_flags: Clear, specific responsibilities tied to measurable outcomes (throughput, utilization, OEE)
Role is part of OpenAI's Robotics team working on general-purpose robotics — high-impact mission
Hands-on floor operations role with defined scope and ownership
Requirements are concrete and experience-based, not vague
red_flags: Requires in-person presence 5 days a week in San Francisco — no remote flexibility
Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international candidates
All requirements phrased under 'You might thrive if you' which is ambiguous about hard vs. soft — treated mostly as hard based on specificity and unconditional language
No explicit mention of equity or total comp despite OpenAI's typical equity-heavy packages

Treated as hard severity except bachelor's degree (explicitly listed as a specific degree, treated as soft given typical JD conventions) and robotics exposure (clearly supplementary).
